error_note: confused indication for amnioinfusion vs surveillance; did not recognize stable BPP (8/10)
takeaway: Oligohydramnios alone with reassuring BPP at 35 weeks -> surveillance, not intervention.  Amnioinfusion is for variable decelerations during labor, not antenatal management.

common_trap: treating milk hyperkalemia with intravenous calcium
error_note: anchored on hyperkalemia and selected intravenous calcium without integrating hyperdynamic left ventricle and arteriovenous fistula
takeaway: hyperdynamic left ventricle with arteriovenous fistula suggests high-output heart failure requiring fistula evaluation rather than hyperkalemia treatment

discriminating_clue: ECG: tall R wave in aVR
error_note: selected phenytoin without recognizing sodium channel blockade pattern requiring sodium bicarbonate
takeaway: seizure with widened QRS and tall R in aVR indicates TCA toxicity requiring sodium bicarbonate rather than standard antiepileptic escalation

error_note: confused cost structure and access limitations of HMO versus PPO for high utilization patient
takeaway: high utilization patients benefit from PPO flexibility while low utilization patients benefit from high deductible plans

common_trap: treating mild hypoxemia with oxygen therapy alone
error_note: selected oxygen therapy without recognizing need for antifibrotic treatment in IPF
takeaway: honeycombing and restrictive PFTs indicate IPD requiring antifibrotic therapy rather than supportive oxygen alone

common_trap: diagnosing cardiac tamponade based on pulsus paradoxus
error_note: confusion between constrictive pericarditis and tamponade physiology
takeaway: post cardiac surgery JVD without inspiratory decline suggests constrictive pericarditis over tamponade

common_trap: anchoring on salpingitis instead of broader PID
error_note: correct answer chosen without fully recognizing PID as unifying diagnosis
takeaway: always rule out pregnancy first in reproductive age women before further management

common_trap: choosing actinomyces due to branching rodes
error_note: failed to distinguish weakly acid fast nocardia from non acid fast actinomyces
takeaway: weakly acid fast branching rods in immunocompromised host indicates nocardia not actinomyces

discriminating_clue: history of cerebrovascular accident due to ischemia
common_trap: choosing carotid stenting instead of endarterectomy
error_note: selected angioplasty instead of surgery for symptomatic high grade stenosis
takeaway: symptomatic carotid stenosis greater than 70% requires endarterectomy not stenting

error_note: chose NSAID despite CKD and CHF contraindications, colchicine also contraindicated
takeaway: acute gout with CKD requires steroids not NSAIDs or colchicine

error_note: focused on DKA instead of uremia indication for dialysis
takeaway: uremic symptoms reuqire emergent dialysis regardless of glucose

error_note: uncertain about management of post obstructive diuresis
takeaway: post obstructive diuresis requires careful fluid reduction to avoid electrolyte imbalance

common_trap: confusing likelihood ratio with odds ratio
error_note: misinterpreted likelihood ratio as odds ratio
takeaway: LR+ > 1 means a person with a positive test result is likely to have the disease

error_note: anchored on psoriasis without considering pain and localization
takeaway: painful sharply demarcated perianal erythema suggests streptococcal infection

confirmatory_test: anti phospholipase A2 receptor antibody testing
next_best_step: anti phospholipase A2 receptor antibody testing
error_note: initial serologic testing for nephrotic syndrome includes PLA2R antibody testing.  biopsy may not need needed if positive
takeaway: nephrotic syndrome with normal complement suggests membranous nephropathy and initial step is PLA2R testing

discriminating_clue: injury while engaged in sports activity
first_intervention: Rest, ice, compression and elevation treatment program
definitive_management: Reconstruction of anterior cruciate ligament of knee joint
error_note: Confused ACL with PCL injury
takeaway: acute hemarthrosis after pivot injury indicates ACL tear, PCL more likely in steering wheel hitting knee in car accident (high energy)

common_trap: Choosing nebulized pentamidine when it is used for prophylaxis
error_note: could not narrow down drugs based on indications
takeaway: PCP in sulfa allergy and G6PD deficiency treated with atovaquone

final_diagnosis: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
error_note: mistook mildly elevated troponins for MI
takeaway: mildly elevated troponins can be seen in HF

error_note: did not linke alcohol, diarrhea, and refractory electrolyte abnormalities to hypomagnesemia
takeaway: hypomagnesemia causes refractory hypocalcemia and hypokalemia and must be corrected first
immunocompromised patient with necrotis sinus tissue and septate hyphae requires urgent amphotericin B

error_note: didn't recognize presentation of guttate psoriasis
takeaway: scaly drop-like rash after strep infection indicates guttate psoriasis

error_note: chose surgery before imaging and antibiotics
takeaway: open skull fracture requires antibiotics before neurosurgical intervention

error_note: selected topiramate despite contraindication
takeaway: refractory migraine after multiple preventive failures requires CGRP inhibitor
fever, murmur, and splinter hemorrhages require echocardiography to confirm endocarditis

discriminating_clue: Circinate balanitis co-occurrent with reactive arthritis triad
confirmatory_test: chlamydia trachomatis nucleic acid amplification test
next_best_step: chlamydia trachomatis nucleic acid amplification test
common_trap: choosing syphilis testing because of painless genital ulcer
error_note: recognized reactive arthritis pattern but switched to syphils based on the painless urethral ulcer
takeaway: circinate balanitis is associated with chlamydia

error_note: Heart failure is less likely w/o JVD or dyspnea, heart sounds are normal
takeaway: In HIV patients with edema and transudative pleural effusions suggests HIV nephropathy from FSGS

error_note: methotrexate can cause tubular necrosis, but the vignette shows nephrotic syndrome
takeaway: NSAIDS can cause secondary nephrotic syndrome in patients with RA

error_note: recognized shock physiology but uncertain between fluid resuscitation in inotropes
takeaway: hypotension with low output and poor response to diuretics in heart failure indicates cardiogenic shock requiring inotropic support

error_note: chose tachycardia instead of diarrhea as side effect of acetylcholinesterase inhibitors
takeaway: acetylcholinesterase inhibitors commonly cause cholinergic side effects such as diarrhea not tachycardia

error_note: missed high frequency short duration pattern distinguishing paroxysmal hemicrania from cluster headache
takeaway: paroxysmal hemicrania presents with frequent short unilateral headaches with autonomic features and responds completely to indomethacin

error_note: failed to recognize cirrhosis requiring phase II benzodiazepine instead of long acting agent
takeaway: in alcohol withdrawal with liver disease use lorazepam, oxazepam, or temazepam due to phase II metabolism and safer hepatic profile

common_trap: anti phospholipase A2 receptor antibody testing
error_note: did not recognize need to evaluate complement in suspected MPGN
takeaway: HCV is associated with MPGN, HBV is associated with membranous nephropathy

discriminating_clue: ultrasound of kidney: multiple cysts of both kidneys
error_note: focused on aneurysm risk instead of hypertension control
takeaway: manage hypertension first in ADPKD before screening complications

error_note: focused on level instead of clinical improvement marker
takeaway: AST level less than half of peak is the best indicator for discontinuation of NAC
